content: This component determines the appropriate complexity level (1-4) for the current task and directs the workflow accordingly.

Complexity is categorized into four levels based on keywords, scope, time, and risk: Level 1 (Quick Bug Fix), Level 2 (Simple Enhancement), Level 3 (Intermediate Feature), and Level 4 (Complex System).
If all checklist items (Task type, Scope, Time, Risk, Dependencies) are identified, proceed with the level-specific workflow; otherwise, the assessment must be completed first.
Level 1 tasks continue in VAN mode, while Level 2-4 tasks trigger a transition to PLAN mode.
For tasks determined to be Level 2, 3, or 4, implementation in VAN mode is BLOCKED.
A task with complexity Level 2, 3, or 4 REQUIRES PLAN mode for proper documentation and planning.
You MUST switch to PLAN mode for tasks with complexity Level 2, 3, or 4.
After the checkpoint verification, if complexity is Level 1, proceed to VAN Mode Completion; if it is Level 2-4, trigger the PLAN Mode transition.
